rationale: The citing paper explicitly positions itself against prior social-media emotionality work focused on message-level outcomes (including review evaluations), and the cited paper is a core example: it studies expressed arousal in reviews and its nonlinear effects on helpfulness with mechanism and hedonic/utilitarian boundary tests. Given this direct conceptual overlap in focal constructs (expressed emotionality/arousal) and the citing paper’s stated refinement of that literature, this is not an obvious incidental mention.
